folio.label: 171r
folio.l: than æè , do the a noye or greuau~ce ; suffre hi~ ,

for he æ oones
hatú greued the , may another tyme releue the & helpe ,

©et
sette I kas , ©e haue bothe mygút & licence , for to venge ©ow ,

I seye , æ æäe be ful manye thynges , æ shuln restreyne ©ow of
vengeau~ce takynge ,

& make ©ow for to enclyne to suffre , & for to
han pacience , in æe wronges , æ han ben doon to ©ow

¢ First and
forward , if ©e wol co~sidre æe defautes , æ ben in ©oure owene ¹-
sone ,

for whicú defautes , god hatú suffred ©ow haue æis tébu-
lacou~ , as I haue seid ©ow heere biforn~

¢ For the Poete seitú ,
æ we ougúten paciently , taken the tébulacou~s , æ comen to vs ,
whan æ we co~sideren , & thynken æ we han desserued to haue
hem

¢ And Seynt Gregorie seitú ; æ whan a man co~sideretú
wel the noumbre , of hise defautes , & of his sy~nes ,

æe peynes
& the tribulaco~ns æ he suffretú , Semen the lesse vn to hi~ ,

And
in as muche as hi~ thynketú hise sy~nes mor heuy & greuous ,

in so muche , semetú his peyne , the ligútere & the esiere vn to
hi~

¢ Also ©e owen to enclyne , & bowe ©oure herte , to take the
pacience of oure lord ihu~ cést , as seitú Seynt Peter in hise epist-
les

¢ Ihu~ cést he seitú hatú suffred for vs , & ©euene ensaumple
to euäy man , to folwe & sewe hi~ ,

for he dide neuäe synne , ne
neuäe cam there , a vileyns word , out of his moutú ,

whan me~
cursed hi~ , he cursed he~ nougút , And whan men betyn hi~ , he
manaced he~ nougút

¢ Also gret pacience , whicú , Seyntes æ
ben in Paradys , han had in tébulacio~ns , æ æei han suffred , w
outen here desert or gilt ,
ougúte muchel Stire ©ow to pacience
¢ Foræämore ©e shulde enforce ©ow , to haue pacience ,

co~sidery~ge
æ the tébulaco~ns of this world , but litel while endure , & soone
passed ben , & gon ,

& the ioye , æ a man seketú to haue , by pacien-
ce in tébulacions , is ¹durable , after æ Thapostle seitú in his
Epistle ,

The ioye of god he seitú , is ¹durable , æ is to seyn ;
euäe lastynge

¢ Also trowetú & bileuetú stedfastly , æ he nys nat
wel I norisshed , ne wel I taugút , æ kan nat haue pacience ,
or wol nat receyue pacience

¢ For Salamon seitú ; the docténe
& wyt of a man , is knowen by pacience

¢ And in a nother pla-
ce he seitú , æ he æ is pacient , gouänetú hi~ bi gret Pèdence

¢ And the same Salamon seitú ; the angry man & wrathful ,
maketú noyses , And the pacient man , attempretú hi~ & stilletú ;

he seitú also ; It is more wortú to be pacient , than for to be
rigút strongö

¢ And he æ may haue the lordshipe , of his owne
herte ; is more to preise , than he æ by his force or strengthe ,
taketú grete Citees

¢ And æäfore seitú Seynt Iame in his
Epistle , that pacience , is a gret vertu of ¹feccou~

¢ Certes
quod Melibe , I gunte ©ow Dame Prudence , æ pacience is crammed line at the end? Double check/ODR/
